senses.definition: third-person singular present of reconvert: to convert again or anew
examples: The material was reconverted to a more durable form.
They reconverts the layout to suit modern needs.

senses.definition: to convey again or back, or to deliver anew, often in a legal or formal context
examples: The property title was reconveyed to the owner.
They reconvey the funds to the originating account.

senses.definition: the act of conveying again, especially transferring property title back to a party
examples: The reconveyance of the deed completed the transfer.
A reconveyance was required to finalize the sale.

senses.definition: plural of reconveyance; acts of conveying again, especially property titles
examples: Several reconveyances were recorded this quarter.
They reviewed all reconveyances filed last year.

senses.definition: past tense of reconvey: conveyed again, especially property title
examples: The title was reconveyed to the seller.
It was reconveyed after the dispute was settled.

senses.definition: present participle of reconvey: conveying again, especially property title
examples: They are reconveying the deed to the new owner.
Reconveying streams the funds back to the lender.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of reconvey: conveys again, especially property title
examples: The lender reconveys the property upon payment.
They reconveys the title to the rightful owner.

senses.definition: to convict again; to declare someone guilty again after an appeal or retrial
examples: The defendant was reconvict after new evidence emerged.
They reconvict the suspect in the retrial.

senses.definition: past tense of reconvict: declared guilty again after retrial or appeal
examples: The defendant was reconvicted after new evidence.
Several suspects were reconvicted in the retrial.

senses.definition: present participle of reconvict: convicting again after retrial or appeal
examples: The court is reconvicting the defendant based on new evidence.
They are reconvicting several defendants in the retrial stage.

senses.definition: the act or process of convicting again; a second or renewed conviction
examples: Her reconviction stunned the defense team.
The case ended in reconviction after new evidence.

senses.definition: plural of reconviction; multiple renewed convictions
examples: The court recorded several reconvictions.
Her various reconvictions were documented.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of reconvict: convicts again after retrial
examples: The court reconvicts offenders when new evidence emerges.
They reconvicts is a common error; use reconvicts with third singular s.

senses.definition: to persuade again; to convince anew
examples: They tried to reconvince the committee with new data.
She attempted to reconvince him of the plan's merits.

senses.definition: past tense of reconvince: persuaded again
examples: He was reconvinced by new evidence.
They reconvinced themselves of the plan.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of reconvince: persuades again
examples: She reconvinces the board with fresh evidence.
The argument reconvinces many skeptics.

senses.definition: present participle of reconvince: persuading again
examples: They are reconvincing the audience with new data.
Reconvincing the client may take time.

senses.definition: to call or summon again, often a meeting or assembly
examples: They decided to reconvoke the council.
The committee was reconvoked to discuss the proposal.

senses.definition: to cook again; to prepare a dish again, sometimes by reheating or reprocessing
examples: We will recook the soup after adjusting the seasoning.
If leftovers are not fresh, recook gently.

senses.definition: past tense of recook: cooked again
examples: The soup was recooked with more spices.
Leftovers were recooked to improve flavor.

senses.definition: present participle of recook: cooking again
examples: Recooking the sauce thickens it.
They are recooking breakfast to feed the crew.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of recook: cooks again
examples: She recooks the vegetables to keep them crisp.
They recooks meals for the guests.

senses.definition: to cool again; to become cooler again or to chill anew
examples: Let the beverage recool before serving.
The room recools after the air is turned off.

senses.definition: someone who cooperates again; a rare or historical term for a person who cooperates
examples: The recooper joined the new alliance.

senses.definition: past tense of recopied: copied again from an original or another copy
examples: The manuscript was recopied for archival purposes.
They recopied the notes to preserve changes.
